root_cause_candidates.cause: Local disk fault or resource saturation on ts-train-service causing CPU/memory pressure and high latency, propagating load to downstream services and degrading their performance.
root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All evidence converges on ts-train-service as the upstream fault. Log Agent highlights severe anomalies on ts-train-service with CPU/memory pressure and latency. Topology shows a structurally plausible path ts-preserve-service → ts-travel-service → ts-train-service, implying ts-train-service is downstream of ts-travel-service but upstream to the symptom path. TCB-RCA, being topology-constrained, did not identify ts-train-service as a root candidate (it prioritized ts-preserve-service with zero temporal gap), thus its result is overridden by stronger log-based and topological signals. This combination supports ts-train-service as the root cause, with the observed symptom at ts-preserve-service via the path ts-train-service → ts-travel-service → ts-preserve-service.

final_verdict.cause: Local disk fault or resource saturation on ts-train-service causing CPU/memory pressure and high latency, propagating load to downstream services and degrading their performance.

notes: Candidate 'ts-train-service' supported by 1 modality (has_log_evidence); confidence capped at 0.6.

agent_results.summary: Incident at ts-preserve-service: 'Service ts-train-service experiencing disk fault'. Errors detected in 2 service(s): ts-food-service (2 errors), ts-ui-dashboard (1 errors). LLM hypothesis: ts-train-service is experiencing a severe CPU and memory pressure (and associated latency) likely due to a local disk fault or resource saturation, which propagates elevated load and delays to downstream services (e.g., ts-food-service, ts-order-service, ts-inside-payment-service) causing cascading performance degradation.
